narrative: Your law school application was denied because our predictive model, which assesses the likelihood of passing the bar exam on the first try, estimated a 49.7% chance that you would not pass—right on the edge, but enough to tip the decision against admission. This model looks at patterns from thousands of past students' academic records, test scores, and backgrounds to forecast bar success, helping the school select applicants most likely to thrive as lawyers. In your case, the strongest factor pulling the prediction toward failure was your Year 3 class rank, which fell in the second decile at your school—much lower than the typical fifth or sixth decile among students who went on to pass the bar. That early struggle in later undergraduate years signaled to the model a potential challenge in mastering the rigorous legal material ahead. On a more positive note, your undergraduate GPA of 3.20 and LSAT score of 37 were close to the averages for bar passers (3.24 and 37.27, respectively), and these helped push the prediction back toward success, showing your foundational strengths. Your high family income and full-time study plans aligned well with successful profiles too, as did being in a group where full-time students make up over 93% of bar passers. Still, that Year 3 decile outweighed those positives as the top influence, followed closely by your GPA and LSAT working in your favor. Compared to admitted students, your profile is solid in test scores and background but stands out for that dip in later-year performance, which the model sees as the biggest risk for bar prep. It's understandable to feel disappointed, especially with your high family support and dedication to full-time study. To move forward, consider retaking courses or building stronger late-stage grades if possible, perhaps through a post-bac program or targeted LSAT refreshers to boost those positive signals. You could also seek feedback from undergrad advisors on what impacted your Year 3 rank and address it head-on. Many students in similar spots have rebounded by focusing on consistent performance—your near-average metrics suggest you're close, and with targeted improvements, a future application could look much stronger. Keep pushing; your determination will shine through.
